items.title: Cohere released an open coding model
items.oneliner: Cohere's 30B MoE coding model activates 3B parameters and claims 2.8x throughput, making local code agents more practical.

items.title: Sakana AI shipped its first product
items.oneliner: Sakana AI moved from research lab to product company with Marlin, a signal that novel architectures now need revenue.

items.title: Meta turned Facebook search into AI answers
items.oneliner: Meta's new Facebook AI Mode answers from posts, groups, and reels, turning social exhaust into a retrieval moat.

items.title: The Fable 5 ban is being challenged
items.oneliner: More than 100 security leaders say banning Fable 5 hurts defenders most, and AI export controls are getting messier.

items.title: Google sued a Gemini-powered scam network
items.oneliner: Google says scammers used Gemini to fuel 2.5 million texts in two weeks, showing AI abuse has real operating scale.

items.title: A fake LinkedIn job led to malware
items.oneliner: A recruiter sent a GitHub repo where npm install triggered a backdoor, so hiring pipelines are now attack surfaces.

items.title: Autonomous drones crossed a deadly threshold
items.oneliner: Ukraine reportedly saw fully autonomous drones kill soldiers, which means human-out-of-the-loop weapons are no longer theoretical.

items.title: Hetzner raised infrastructure prices
items.oneliner: Hetzner's June 15 price adjustment is a reminder that inference economics still depend on stubborn hardware realities.

items.title: Iroh reached version 1.0
items.oneliner: Iroh 1.0 says devices should be addressed by keys, not IPs, and 200 million endpoints suggest the idea is landing.

items.title: Trinket was rescued by the community
items.oneliner: Trinket shut down, then got revived as a free community host, which is a useful reminder to avoid platform dependence.
